page: Amazon Web Services c5a.24xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by dedicated AMD EPYC 7R32 processors using the x86_64 architecture. It features 96 virtual CPUs across 48 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and providing a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The instance exhibits poor single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the bottom 10%. However, it demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities, with a top 10% tier performance in PassMark CPU encryption tests and top 25% performance in PassMark CPU score, physics tests, and extended instructions. Memory performance is highly efficient for cached operations, achieving a strong read-only small block bandwidth of 4497.3 GB/sec, though uncached large block read and write speeds remain average.

Given its high core count and strong multi-threaded execution, the c5a.24xlarge is well-suited for parallel processing tasks such as software compilation, ray tracing, image processing, and high-throughput Redis SET operations, where it scores in the top 25%. It also handles moderate LLM inference prompt processing for smaller models efficiently. However, workloads dependent on high single-thread performance or local high-speed storage may experience bottlenecks. This server profile offers a cost-efficient option for compute-heavy, highly parallelized workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services c5a.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor running at 3.3 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory but lacks local storage and GPU hardware. While single-core performance is limited, placing in the bottom 10% in stress-ng tests, the instance delivers strong multi-core and cryptographic capabilities, scoring in the top 10% for PassMark CPU encryption. It also achieves strong results in software compilation, ray tracing, and Redis operations, supported by a high cached memory read bandwidth of 4497.3 GB/sec. With a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps, this instance is highly suited for parallelized compute workloads, software development pipelines, and memory-cached database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5a.24xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 96 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and AMD EPYC 7R32 processors, delivering strong multi-core performance and high-speed encryption capabilities.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5a.24xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and dedicated AMD EPYC processors.
tagline: A high-core-count compute instance optimized for parallel processing, cryptographic workloads, and memory-cached operations without local storage.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 192 GB DDR4 RAM powered by AMD EPYC 7R32 processors.
Top-tier performance in CPU encryption and strong multi-core benchmark results.
No local storage or GPU hardware, utilizing network-attached storage.
20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the Nitro hypervisor.
Optimal for parallel workloads, software compilation, and Redis operations.
